input.background:
As part of a study into the effects of woodland management on bird distribution, 36 woods were surveyed for the presence (P) or absence (A) of long-tailed tits and classified as one of three woodland types: pre-thicket (PT), thicket (T) or high forest (HF). The following data were obtained:

\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|l|l|}
\hline Wood number & Wood type & Presence or absence of longtailed tits & Wood number & Wood type & Presence or absence of longtailed tits \\
\hline 1 & PT & P & 19 & HF & A \\
\hline 2 & HF & P & 20 & T & P \\
\hline 3 & PT & A & 21 & PT & P \\
\hline 4 & PT & P & 22 & PT & P \\
\hline 5 & HF & A & 23 & HF & A \\
\hline 6 & T & P & 24 & T & P \\
\hline 7 & HF & A & 25 & PT & A \\
\hline 8 & T & P & 26 & PT & P \\
\hline 9 & HF & P & 27 & HF & P \\
\hline 10 & T & P & 28 & T & P \\
\hline 11 & PT & A & 29 & PT & A \\
\hline 12 & T & A & 30 & PT & P \\
\hline 13 & T & P & 31 & T & P \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|l|l|}
\hline Wood number & Wood type & Presence or absence of longtailed tits & Wood number & Wood type & Presence or absence of longtailed tits \\
\hline 14 & HF & P & 32 & HF & A \\
\hline 15 & PT & A & 33 & T & P \\
\hline 16 & T & P & 34 & HF & A \\
\hline 17 & HF & A & 35 & PT & P \\
\hline 18 & HF & P & 36 & HF & A \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
input.question: What do you conclude regarding the distribution of long-tailed tits in the different types of woodland?

output.answer: There is a significant association between forest type and the presence or absence of long-tailed tits, with tits being more likely to be present in thickets (and absent from high forests).

meta_info.book: Using Statistics to Understand the Environment
